appended_follow_up_ticket.title: P06e external peak-phase causality control
appended_follow_up_ticket.body: Question: does the P06d peak/CFD-phase coupling burden persist in an external control that breaks same-event pulse physics, such as forced-random/no-pulse ROOT where available or A-stack/B-stack event-matched controls? Expected information gain: distinguishes a causal waveform atom from acquisition/run-family confounding after the P06d phase-gated CNN win. Compare a support-matched peak-phase atlas against ridge, gradient-boosted trees, MLP, 1D-CNN, and the phase-gated CNN with run-held-out bootstrap CIs and shuffled/phase-dropout sentinels.

pre_registered.primary_metric: held-out Sample-II MAE for the standardized peak-phase coupling burden, evaluated on B4/B6/B8 pulses in events with at least two selected timing staves
pre_registered.split: train on Sample I runs, calibrate scalar offsets on run 64 only, evaluate only Sample II analysis runs 58-63 and 65; all confidence intervals resample held-out runs
pre_registered.traditional_method: support-matched median atlas in stave, amplitude, peak-sample, and CFD-phase bins
pre_registered.win_rule: lowest held-out MAE; ML wins only if its paired run-block bootstrap CI for method minus traditional is entirely below zero
